Decision: Center version 1 on one active product
Context: The product could become a broad life and business dashboard.
Options Considered: One active product; multi-product portfolio; full second brain
Chosen Direction: Design the default experience around one active product while keeping relations multi-product capable.
Why: A narrow default reduces setup friction and makes the weekly workflow clearer.
Tradeoff: Advanced users may want portfolio-level reporting sooner.
Evidence Needed Next: Observe whether testers create additional products without guidance.

Decision: Keep content planning out of the core dashboard
Context: Content planning is adjacent to launch work but can dominate the workspace.
Options Considered: Full editorial calendar; launch-only content checklist; no content support
Chosen Direction: Include launch tasks in the Launch Room without adding a full editorial database.
Why: The core promise is product operating rhythm, not social media management.
Tradeoff: Some buyers may expect a content calendar.
Evidence Needed Next: Track waitlist questions and tester requests.

Context: The product page currently shows a planned $49 price.
Chosen Direction: Test a $39 early-access price before moving to $49.
Why: A lower first price creates room for feedback while preserving a premium template position.
Tradeoff: Early buyers may anchor to the lower price.
Evidence Needed Next: Compare conversion and support load after ten sales.
